content: JSON.parse('{"__proto__":...}') on its own does NOT pollute Object.prototype -- it creates an own property literally named __proto__ on the parsed object. The real danger is downstream: recursive merge/clone/assign utilities (deep merge, lodash merge/set, custom import-normalizer loops using obj[key]=val) that treat __proto__/constructor/prototype keys as normal keys and walk into the real prototype chain. Any import pipeline that merges parsed JSON into existing objects (e.g. character or plugin normalization) must block those three key names explicitly.
